message:
chore: update issue tracker bead statuses

Minor status field updates in .beads/issues.jsonl reflecting ongoing
concurrent agent progress on asupersync migration beads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nk5","title":"[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Default templates block unsafe internal destinations; explicit allowlist exceptions require policy receipts; regression tests cover common SSRF patterns.

Expected Artifacts:
- `config/policies/network_guard_default.toml`, `tests/security/ssrf_default_deny.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/ssrf_policy_test_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-1nk5/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-1nk5/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Add SSRF-deny default policy template (localhost/tailnet/private CIDR denied unless explicitly allowed)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:52.366422143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:28:37.880582192Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:28:37.880556835Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nk5","depends_on_id":"bd-2m2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:12.756576197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl1","title":"[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Speculative transforms cannot activate without proof receipts and guard checks; guard failure always degrades to deterministic safe baseline with no correctness regression; activation occurs only via approved franken_engine interfaces.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/proof_carrying_speculation.md`, `src/runtime/speculation/proof_executor.rs`, `tests/conformance/proof_speculation_guards.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/speculation_proof_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1nl1/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1nl1/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Build proof-carrying speculative execution governance framework for extension-host hot paths." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Speculative transforms cannot activate without proof receipts and guard checks; guard failure always degrades to deterministic safe baseline with no correctness regression; activation occurs only via approved franken_engine interfaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkLantern","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:02.927493339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T04:14:51.994167070Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T04:14:51.994141452Z","close_reason":"Proof-carrying speculation governance delivered with guard/fallback contract and verification artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:33.798018723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-229","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:33.843240390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-3il","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:33.888249500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:33.933893504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-5rh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:33.978872949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:34.023526217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl1","depends_on_id":"bd-n71","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:34.070551974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nmg","title":"Epic: CI/CD Pipeline Setup","description":"- type: task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:47:58.072163029Z","updated_at":"2026-02-20T07:49:21.083126433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T07:49:21.083109191Z","close_reason":"Superseded by canonical detailed master-plan graph (bd-33v) with full 10.N-10.21 and 11-16 coverage, explicit dependencies, and per-section verification gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1now","title":"[runtime] Selective Asupersync leverage plan for franken_node","description":"Why this exists:
A focused architecture audit on 2026-03-09 asked whether `franken_node` should leverage Asupersync more aggressively for materially better performance, correctness, and robustness.

What the audit established:
- `frankenengine-node` had a dead Tokio bootstrap path: direct `tokio` dependency plus `#[tokio::main]` in `crates/franken-node/src/main.rs`, with no meaningful `.await` usage in the crate. That cleanup is already completed in `bd-1now.2`.
- The broader system already consumes canonical Asupersync primitives indirectly through `frankenengine-engine`, so node-side migration must be justified by real leverage rather than aesthetic consistency.
- Most node-side Asupersync-flavored surfaces are currently semantic/model/spec layers, not live async runtime boundaries.
- The telemetry ingestion seam (`TelemetryBridge` + `EngineDispatcher`) is the one place where explicit ownership, bounded admission, shutdown, failure handling, and operator-grade diagnostics can materially improve correctness and robustness for users.

Strategic decision encoded by this epic:
- Do not launch a broad crate-wide Asupersync migration.
- Remove dead executor scaffolding and add explicit anti-regression guardrails.
- Concentrate runtime-ownership work on the telemetry ingestion seam.
- Define a semantic-boundary policy so local semantic twins do not drift into accidental forks of upstream-owned canonical primitives.
- Encode deferred triggers for future request-boundary and actor-style adoption so future work is driven by real topology.
- Make verification first-class: every immediate implementation lane must spell out its unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations so users/operators get evidence rather than promises.

User-facing outcomes this epic is trying to buy:
- no silent reintroduction of ambient executor scaffolding,
- no ambiguous telemetry shutdown/failure behavior,
- explicit and diagnosable overflow/backpressure behavior for operators,
- preserved determinism and replay/audit quality,
- clearer ownership boundaries between local node concepts and canonical upstream Asupersync primitives.

Non-goals:
- Do not rewrite the whole crate around Asupersync right now.
- Do not force native Asupersync service/request-region APIs onto `api/service.rs` before this crate owns a real async HTTP or gRPC boundary.
- Do not replace every `Mutex`/singleton with actors for stylistic purity.
- Do not duplicate canonical upstream primitive ownership already centralized in `frankenengine-engine` without an explicit reason.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The tracker contains a complete, self-contained implementation graph for selective Asupersync leverage in `franken_node`, including immediate work, deferred trigger conditions, verification gates, and the reasoning that led to those choices.
2. The graph distinguishes clearly between: (a) immediate high-confidence cleanup, (b) likely high-value runtime-ownership refactors, (c) semantic-boundary policy work, and (d) future migrations that should remain deferred until the crate grows the required topology.
3. Every immediate implementation lane includes explicit proof obligations: deterministic unit tests, end-to-end scripts where runtime behavior is user-visible, schema/version-stable structured logging and artifact requirements, and baseline-comparison obligations strong enough for postmortem diagnosis.
4. The graph captures enough architectural context that future contributors do not need to reopen this chat or any external markdown plan to understand why these tasks exist, what problem they solve, and why broader migration is intentionally constrained.
5. The graph encodes that any heavy cargo build/check/clippy/test validation for this epic must be run through `rch`, not on the local workstation.","notes":"Audit evidence that motivated this epic:
- `crates/franken-node/src/main.rs`: former `#[tokio::main]` bootstrap, removed in `bd-1now.2` after audit confirmed no real `.await` dependence.
- `crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml` and workspace `Cargo.toml`: former direct Tokio footprint used only for dead bootstrap scaffolding.
- `crates/franken-node/src/ops/telemetry_bridge.rs`: listener built from `thread::spawn`, nested per-connection threads, and `Arc<Mutex<FrankensqliteAdapter>>`.
- `crates/franken-node/src/ops/engine_dispatcher.rs`: telemetry bridge started as a side effect before engine process dispatch, without explicit stop/join ownership surfaced to the caller.
- `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane/mod.rs`: canonical control-plane adapter already imports upstream `/dp/asupersync` primitives (`Cx`, `Budget`, `TraceId`, etc.), so additional node-side migration must prove its value.
- `crates/franken-node/src/connector/region_ownership.rs`, `crates/franken-node/src/connector/supervision.rs`, and `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/region_tree.rs`: local semantic twins exist and need explicit policy, not accidental drift.

Planning rules preserved here:
- prefer surgical leverage over symbolic migration,
- treat operator-visible diagnostics as a product requirement, not a nice-to-have,
- require `rch` for heavy validation,
- close the epic only after the dedicated verification gate produces machine-readable and human-readable evidence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:39.683448788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T04:29:49.316355694Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","plan","planning","runtime","self-contained","test-obligations","verification-gate"]}

- `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane/mod.rs`: canonical control-plane adapter already imports upstream `/dp/asupersync` primitives (`Cx`, `Budget`, `TraceId`, etc.), so additional node-side migration must prove its value.
- `crates/franken-node/src/connector/region_ownership.rs`, `crates/franken-node/src/connector/supervision.rs`, and `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/region_tree.rs`: local semantic twins exist and need explicit policy, not accidental drift.

Planning rules preserved here:
- prefer surgical leverage over symbolic migration,
- treat operator-visible diagnostics as a product requirement, not a nice-to-have,
- require `rch` for heavy validation,
- close the epic only after the dedicated verification gate produces machine-readable and human-readable evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"TurquoiseReef","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:39.683448788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T16:51:40.284164222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","plan","planning","runtime","self-contained","test-obligations","verification-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.1","title":"Document selective Asupersync leverage baseline and decision boundaries","description":"Why this exists:
Before any runtime change lands, the project needs a self-contained record of what the 2026-03-09 audit actually found and what it did not find. Without that, future work risks turning into cargo-cult migration or re-litigating the same decision repeatedly.

What this task must do:
Create the canonical decision record for selective Asupersync leverage in `franken_node`.

The record must capture:
- the exact direct Tokio footprint in `frankenengine-node` at audit time,
- the exact indirect Asupersync footprint already entering through `frankenengine-engine`,
- the difference between local model/spec layers and live runtime boundaries,
- the reason the telemetry listener path was selected as the main high-value runtime seam,
- the explicit reasons broad migration of `api/service.rs` and similar skeletons is deferred,
- the criteria future contributors must use before widening Asupersync adoption.

Why this matters:
This bead is the anchor that keeps future implementation work from silently drifting into “replace things because they look old” instead of “replace only where ownership/lifecycle semantics buy correctness.”","acceptance_criteria":"1. The decision record inventories the direct Tokio footprint and confirms whether the executor shell is functionally dead or still required.
2. The decision record inventories the indirect canonical Asupersync imports already present upstream in `frankenengine-engine` and explains why that existing leverage changes the adoption strategy in `franken_node`.
3. The decision record classifies relevant local surfaces into at least: live runtime seam, local semantic/model layer, skeleton/future boundary, and deferred migration candidate.
4. The record explicitly names the telemetry ingestion path as the main immediate refactor candidate and explains why other surfaces are deferred.
5. The record is self-contained and detailed enough that future contributors can make adoption decisions without consulting this chat.","notes":"Important background to preserve:
- This is not a neutral “survey every Asupersync feature” task. It is a selective-leverage decision task.
- The bar for recommending broader adoption is “blatantly obvious and materially accretive for performance/correctness/robustness,” not “technically possible.”
- If this task concludes that some surfaces should remain local models or be explicitly deferred, that is a valid success case.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyCanyon","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.078802649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:06:19.305960725Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:06:19.287615108Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","audit","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.078802649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.2","title":"Remove dead Tokio bootstrap from frankenengine-node CLI","description":"Why this exists:
The audit found an explicit `tokio` dependency and `#[tokio::main]` bootstrap in `frankenengine-node`, but no `.await` sites anywhere in the crate. That strongly suggests Tokio is currently dead executor scaffolding rather than a necessary runtime boundary.

What this task must do:
Remove the dead Tokio bootstrap from the `frankenengine-node` CLI if the audit confirmation still holds.

Scope:
- Prove the crate really does not require Tokio for any live async behavior.
- Convert the CLI bootstrap to a synchronous `main` if that proof holds.
- Remove the direct `tokio` dependency from `crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml` and the workspace root if this workspace no longer needs it.
- Ensure the resulting bootstrap remains straightforward and does not introduce a fake local executor abstraction just to preserve shape.

Why this matters:
This is the lowest-risk, highest-confidence cleanup in the audit. It removes ambient runtime machinery that currently adds conceptual noise without buying structured concurrency, performance, or correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The crate either proves Tokio is still needed and documents the exact surviving requirement, or it removes the Tokio bootstrap entirely.
2. If removed, `crates/franken-node/src/main.rs` no longer uses `#[tokio::main]`, and the crate compiles/runs without direct Tokio bootstrap support.
3. `crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml` and workspace `Cargo.toml` no longer carry Tokio solely for this dead bootstrap path.
4. Validation demonstrates that no hidden `.await`/Tokio runtime dependence was accidentally masked by the refactor.
5. The resulting change is framed explicitly as dead-runtime cleanup, not as “Asupersync migration complete.”","notes":"This task should stay small and surgical. If investigation discovers a real async requirement, stop pretending this is trivial and document the blocker clearly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyCanyon","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.473235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:11:28.397104594Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:11:28.397070911Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","runtime","tokio-cleanup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.473235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:45.097671102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.3","title":"Add guardrail against ambient Tokio/runtime reintroduction","description":"Why this exists:
`bd-1now.2` removed the dead Tokio bootstrap, but that cleanup will decay unless the repo encodes a concrete anti-regression rule. This cluster turns the runtime-boundary rule into something contributors can see, run, and understand.

What this cluster must do:
- implement a deterministic guardrail against unjustified direct Tokio bootstrap/runtime reintroduction inside `frankenengine-node`,
- make the architectural exception path explicit instead of forcing future contributors to guess how legitimate async-boundary work should be introduced,
- add both unit-level and end-to-end negative fixtures so the guardrail is proved rather than assumed,
- ensure failure output is detailed enough to tell developers exactly what tripped, why it is banned, and what architectural work is required before the ban may be relaxed.

Why this matters:
Without a real checker plus proof harness, ambient executor scaffolding tends to creep back one convenience macro at a time. Users do not benefit from that drift, and future maintainers lose the audit trail that justified the cleanup in the first place.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The repo has a deterministic guardrail that fails when `frankenengine-node` reintroduces unjustified Tokio bootstrap/runtime scaffolding.
2. The exception path is documented and precise: a real async boundary, updated decision record, and an intentionally reviewed relaxation mechanism rather than an ad hoc reintroduction.
3. The guardrail has dedicated proof coverage: unit tests for true-positive / false-positive boundaries, deterministic negative and allowed-case end-to-end fixtures, and reusable machine-readable plus human-readable artifacts.
4. Failure output is specific enough for contributors to act on immediately, including the offending pattern/surface and the policy reason.
5. Any heavy validation needed by this cluster uses `rch` rather than local cargo execution.","notes":"This cluster is intentionally split into implementation and proof. The goal is not “ban Tokio forever”; the goal is “ban ambient executor creep unless the crate truly grows a boundary that warrants it.”

Keep the checker cheap enough to run routinely, but do not trade away precision. False positives that punish legitimate future async-boundary work will cause contributors to route around the guardrail instead of respecting it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"WindyDesert","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.876029147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:12:07.189726035Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:12:07.189696610Z","close_reason":"Verified live guardrail, blueprint exception path, 8 unit tests, and deterministic E2E evidence; parent acceptance satisfied","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","plan","runtime","self-contained","test-obligations","tokio-cleanup","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:40.876029147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:45.302404126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:45.507463948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -214,7 +214,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1now.4.7","title":"Characterize telemetry throughput, backpressure budgets, and operator-facing performance evidence","description":"Why this exists:
The telemetry refactor is justified partly on robustness and correctness, but users also care whether the new ownership model preserves or improves throughput, burst handling, and drain behavior. Without explicit performance evidence, the project could “clean up” the runtime story while silently regressing real operator experience.

What this task must do:
Characterize the refactored telemetry path under realistic load and backpressure conditions, then publish operator-usable performance evidence.

The characterization should cover at minimum:
- steady-state ingestion behavior,
- burst behavior up to and beyond the declared queue/admission budget,
- drain/shutdown time after stop/join,
- queue depth/capacity evolution,
- enqueue/dequeue / persist latency signals,
- any shed/block/batch/fail behavior that the design selected.

Required outputs:
- repeatable load/performance harnesses using the implemented telemetry scripts/logging,
- machine-readable summaries,
- human-readable operator notes explaining what the measured budgets mean in practice,
- stable artifact names that the closeout gate can reference.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The telemetry cluster has repeatable performance/load evidence covering steady-state, burst, overflow/backpressure, and drain/shutdown behavior.
2. The evidence is produced with `rch`-offloaded heavy execution rather than local cargo runs.
3. Machine-readable summaries capture the key user-visible signals: queue depth/capacity, latency or service-time indicators, overflow decisions, drain time, and event-accounting totals needed to interpret throughput without hiding loss; those summaries also carry stable schema/version markers plus scenario identifiers.
4. Human-readable operator notes explain the practical meaning of the measured budgets/thresholds, tradeoffs, and any divergence between ideal throughput and durable persistence guarantees, including any before/after change versus the pre-refactor baseline or clearly declared predecessor behavior; if any budgets are configurable, the notes also explain recommended tuning ranges and consequences, and if they are intentionally fixed, the notes explain why.
5. If the measurements materially contradict the intended design envelope, the bead remains open until the deviation is explained or the implementation/design is corrected.","notes":"This is not a benchmark vanity bead. It exists so the refactor has explicit evidence that it did not trade correctness improvements for unacceptable user-visible throughput or shutdown regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.303723986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T09:31:07.651538735Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T09:31:07.651510212Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","performance","rch","runtime","self-contained","telemetry","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.303723986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.697921814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.4.8","title":"Publish telemetry operator troubleshooting guide and reason-code reference","description":"Why this exists:
Detailed logs and machine-readable summaries only improve user/operator experience if humans can quickly interpret them during incidents or regression triage. The telemetry refactor therefore needs an explicit operator-facing reference aligned to the new states, reason codes, and artifact bundles.

What this task must do:
Publish an operator-facing telemetry troubleshooting/reference guide aligned to the refactored telemetry lifecycle.

The guide should cover at minimum:
- lifecycle states and what they mean,
- overflow/backpressure decision codes and expected operator responses,
- accepted/persisted/drained/dropped accounting fields and how to read them,
- where the E2E, performance, and verification artifacts live,
- common failure modes and the first diagnostic steps to take.

Required outputs:
- a stable docs surface in the repo,
- references that match the actual structured fields and reason codes emitted by the implementation and proof harnesses,
- concise troubleshooting steps that future operators/contributors can follow without reconstructing the architecture from scratch.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The telemetry cluster has an operator-facing reference that maps lifecycle states, reason codes, accounting fields, relevant fixed-vs-configurable budgets/tuning knobs, and artifact locations to concrete interpretation/action.
2. The reference is aligned to the actual machine-readable fields and logs emitted by the proof harnesses rather than aspirational names.
3. The guide covers normal, degraded, and failure scenarios, including overflow/backpressure and abnormal termination.
4. The guide explains how to distinguish policy-authorized shedding, delayed durability, or bounded drain behavior from unexpected loss, duplication, or stuck shutdown using accounting fields, reason codes, and performance-envelope artifacts.
5. The closeout gate can point to this reference as part of the reusable evidence pack for future maintainers/operators.","notes":"This is not generic documentation polish. It is part of the user-facing robustness story: logs are only valuable if operators can decode them quickly under pressure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:37.818221975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T09:32:31.776811476Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T09:32:31.776783854Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","runtime","self-contained","telemetry","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:37.818221975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:38.225874797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:38.426406261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.5","title":"Decide local semantic twin policy versus canonical franken_engine/asupersync adapters","description":"Why this exists:
`franken_node` contains local semantic twins of several Asupersync-flavored concepts while `frankenengine-engine` already centralizes canonical upstream primitive imports. If the repo does not define that boundary explicitly, future contributors will oscillate between duplicate-type sprawl and premature migration churn.

What this cluster must do:
- inventory the local semantic twins and classify each one against the canonical upstream ownership boundary,
- explain why each surface should stay local, wrap canonical primitives, migrate only when a live runtime boundary exists, or be forbidden as a duplicate fork,
- add anti-drift proof obligations so the policy remains actionable instead of decaying into prose.

Why this matters:
This is where the project decides what belongs locally for modeling/spec reasons versus what should ultimately collapse onto upstream-owned primitives. The output must be self-contained enough that future maintainers can make consistent decisions without reopening this chat.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The repo has an explicit inventory of local Asupersync-like semantic twins in `franken_node`.
2. Each inventoried surface has a clear policy outcome: keep local, wrap canonical, migrate later under trigger conditions, or forbid as a duplicate primitive fork.
3. The cluster includes anti-drift safeguards, repository-level validation scripts, detailed versioned logging/artifacts, and explicit mapping from policy entries to enforcement/proof surfaces so the policy is enforceable rather than purely narrative.
4. The policy preserves the existing upstream ownership boundary in `frankenengine-engine` and gives future contributors a reliable decision procedure for new concepts, including how to find the rule/check/script that enforces or exemplifies the chosen classification.
5. Any heavy validation needed by this cluster is routed through `rch`.","notes":"This is a semantic-boundary cluster, not a mandate to rewrite everything. A successful outcome may explicitly keep several node-side surfaces local. The important thing is to make those decisions principled, testable where feasible, and reusable by future contributors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:43.827371396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T04:29:50.618078304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","plan","runtime","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:43.827371396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:47.157217855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1now.5.1","title":"Inventory and classify node-side semantic twins against canonical upstream boundaries","description":"Why this exists:
Before the repo can defend a boundary policy, it needs an explicit inventory and classification matrix for the local semantic twins already present.

What this task must do:
Inventory the relevant node-side semantic twins and classify each one against canonical upstream ownership.

The inventory must cover at least:
- `connector/region_ownership.rs`,
- `connector/supervision.rs`,
- `runtime/region_tree.rs`,
- performance budget guards,
- any additional node-side semantic twin discovered while implementing the matrix.

For each surface, capture:
- what problem it solves locally,
- which upstream canonical primitive or adapter it relates to (if any),
- whether it should stay local, wrap canonical ownership, migrate only under a future trigger, or be treated as forbidden duplicate/fork surface,
- what user/operator consequence exists if the surface drifts semantically.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The inventory/classification matrix covers the known node-side semantic twins and any additional relevant surfaces found during implementation.
2. Each entry contains a concrete policy outcome plus the reasoning behind it.
3. Each entry records the user/operator or contributor-facing consequence of semantic drift so the classification is grounded in behavior rather than aesthetics.
4. The matrix includes representative decision examples future contributors can analogize from, including at least one “keep local” case and one “must not fork / should align upstream” case.
5. The matrix is self-contained enough that future contributors can classify a new similar surface by analogy instead of improvising.
6. Each matrix entry points to the intended enforcement/proof surface (checker, test, script, or an explicit justification for why the entry is document-only for now) so the policy is operational rather than merely classificatory.","notes":"Bias toward explicit reasoning over short labels. The goal is for future maintainers to understand why a surface stays local or moves upstream, not just to see a terse classification word.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleCave","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T04:55:23.222374269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

The goal is for future maintainers to understand why a surface stays local or moves upstream, not just to see a terse classification word.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmSnow","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T16:49:22.928556269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.5.2","title":"Add anti-drift safeguards, examples, and consistency checks for semantic-boundary policy","description":"Why this exists:
A classification matrix without anti-drift proof obligations will decay as soon as new local helpers, wrappers, or canonical imports get added under delivery pressure.

What this task must do:
Add the anti-drift layer for the semantic-boundary policy.

Depending on the surfaces involved, that anti-drift layer may include:
- deterministic unit tests or snapshot tests that protect key local-vs-canonical classification tables,
- examples / contributor-facing decision cases that show when a new concept stays local versus when it must reference canonical upstream ownership,
- cheap checkers that fail when a forbidden duplicate/fork pattern or undocumented boundary crossing reappears,
- stable failure logging or reason codes so policy violations are easy to triage.

Why this matters:
This is what makes the semantic-boundary policy reusable and enforceable rather than a one-time document.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The semantic-boundary policy has deterministic anti-drift safeguards appropriate to the actual surfaces involved (tests, snapshots, examples, checkers, or a justified combination).
2. The safeguards make it obvious when a contributor is creating an undocumented duplicate fork or boundary crossing.
3. Failure output is detailed enough for contributors to understand what changed and which policy rule they violated.
4. The safeguards export stable rule ids/messages and, where applicable, machine-readable fields that the repo-level proof harness can reuse instead of inventing a second policy vocabulary.
5. This bead delivers the implementation-layer safeguards; the repo-level scripted/E2E proof lane is tracked separately.
6. If cargo-heavy validation is needed, it is executed through `rch`.","notes":"Do not overfit this to one current file layout. The anti-drift layer should help future contributors make correct ownership decisions even if files move or additional local semantic surfaces appear.

Keep the implementation-layer checks composable so the later repo-level proof harness can reuse the same rule ids, messages, and artifacts instead of inventing parallel policy logic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.853428848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T22:47:20.828695255Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","self-contained","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.853428848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:48.264057922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.5.3","title":"Add repo-level semantic-boundary proof harness with E2E cases and detailed logs","description":"Why this exists:
The semantic-boundary policy will not stay trustworthy if it only exists as a matrix plus low-level checks. The repo needs a scripted proof lane that demonstrates both forbidden and allowed cases with detailed, reusable diagnostics.

What this task must do:
Add a repository-level validation harness for the semantic-boundary policy, with deterministic positive and negative cases.

The harness should exercise at minimum:
- a forbidden duplicate/fork case that must fail,
- an undocumented boundary crossing case that must fail,
- at least one explicitly allowed local-model/spec case that must pass,
- at least one canonical-alignment case that must pass or route to the documented upstream rule.

Required outputs:
- at least one deterministic script under `tests/e2e/` or a tightly justified equivalent repo-level harness,
- machine-readable summary output,
- human-readable detailed logs/artifacts,
- stable reason codes / rule ids that map directly back to the semantic-boundary policy.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The semantic-boundary cluster has deterministic repository-level validation coverage for both forbidden and allowed cases.
2. The harness emits both machine-readable summaries and human-readable detailed logs/artifacts.
3. Failures identify the violated boundary rule with stable reason codes / rule ids and enough context for contributors to fix the issue without policy archaeology.
4. Machine-readable outputs carry stable case identifiers, schema/version markers, and artifact locations that map back to the semantic-boundary matrix and anti-drift rule ids.
5. The harness is reusable by the closeout gate and future regressions, not a one-off proof script.
6. Any cargo-heavy or broad validation required by this lane is executed through `rch`.","notes":"Treat this like a policy E2E lane, not a documentation demo. The point is to prove that the boundary policy can catch real mistakes and preserve intentionally local surfaces without confusing contributors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.252355123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T22:47:21.028884411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","e2e","rch","self-contained","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.252355123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.683187486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.6","title":"Deferred trigger: adopt native Asupersync request-region boundaries when a real async server lands","description":"Why this exists:
The audit deliberately rejected an immediate native-Asupersync migration of the control-plane service skeleton because `crates/franken-node/src/api/service.rs` is still a skeleton/catalog layer rather than a live async HTTP or gRPC server boundary.

What this deferred task must preserve for future self:
If `franken_node` later grows a real async network boundary, that is the moment to revisit native Asupersync request-region/capability-narrowing patterns.

Future trigger conditions:
- real socket accept loop or gRPC call boundary is introduced in this crate,
- request cancellation and per-request lifecycle become real runtime concerns,
- middleware begins owning spawn/time/IO authority rather than just metadata,
- the crate needs deterministic request shutdown/drain semantics.

When the trigger fires, the future implementation should evaluate:
- request-region ownership,
- narrowed request `Cx` capability exposure,
- explicit lifecycle/Outcome mapping at transport boundaries,
- whether the crate should own an `AppSpec`-like supervised service topology.

Why this bead exists now:
It prevents future contributors from forgetting that the right answer for HTTP/gRPC boundaries may eventually be native Asupersync — while also making clear that today is not that day.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The deferred bead captures the exact trigger conditions that would justify revisiting native Asupersync request/call boundary patterns in `franken_node`.
2. The bead makes it explicit that the current service skeleton is not yet sufficient justification for a migration.
3. Future contributors can pick this bead up without needing to rediscover why the work was deferred, what proof obligations wake it up, and what evidence shape the eventual cluster must produce.","notes":"This bead should remain deferred until the crate owns a real async server boundary. Treat premature activation as a design smell.

When this bead is eventually activated, the very first execution move should be a fresh design + verification cluster that includes:
- request-boundary ownership/lifecycle design,
- deterministic unit tests for boundary semantics,
- transport-level end-to-end scripts,
- structured lifecycle logging/artifacts with stable schema/version markers,
- baseline comparison against predecessor transport behavior where relevant,
- operator/contributor guidance for any exposed tuning or backpressure budgets,
- `rch`-offloaded heavy cargo validation.","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:44.260873625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T04:29:50.832026969Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","deferred-trigger","grpc","http","runtime","self-contained"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:44.260873625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:57.353548031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
